activities_projects.title: Natural Indicator Test
activities_projects.objective: Test various household substances using natural indicators
steps: Apply turmeric paste on white paper strips and let them dry
Put a drop of lemon juice on one strip and observe the color
Put a drop of soap solution on another strip and observe the color
Note which substance turns the yellow turmeric red
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that bases change the color of turmeric to red, while acids do not
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the color changes for both substances (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Litmus Paper Identification
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between acids and bases using litmus paper
steps: Place a drop of HCl on both red and blue litmus papers
Place a drop of NaOH on both red and blue litmus papers
Record the final color of each paper
Summarize the 'Blue to Red' and 'Red to Blue' rules
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the standard laboratory test for identifying acidic and basic nature
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the observation table for litmus tests (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reaction of Acid with Metal
activities_projects.objective: Observe the gas produced when an acid reacts with a metal
steps: Take 5ml of dilute sulphuric acid in a test tube
Add a few zinc granules to it
Observe the bubbles of gas forming on the zinc surface
Bring a burning matchstick near the mouth of the test tube
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that acids react with metals to produce hydrogen gas
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of the 'pop' sound produced by the gas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Acids and Carbonates
activities_projects.objective: Observe the reaction between baking soda and lemon juice
steps: Take a pinch of baking soda in a glass
Squeeze lemon juice over it
Observe the brisk effervescence
Discuss which gas is causing the bubbling
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that metal carbonates/hydrogencarbonates release carbon dioxide with acids
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the gas evolved ($CO_2$) (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The pH Scale Chart
activities_projects.objective: Relate common substances to their approximate pH values
steps: Draw a pH scale from 0 to 14
Color the scale from red (acidic) to violet (basic)
Mark the position of items: Gastric juice, Lemon juice, Pure water, Milk of Magnesia, $NaOH$
Label the 'Neutral' point on the scale
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the strength of acids and bases based on the pH value
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct placement of substances on the pH scale (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Neutralization in a Test Tube
activities_projects.objective: Observe the reaction between an acid and a base
steps: Take 2ml of $NaOH$ and add 2 drops of phenolphthalein (it turns pink)
Add dilute $HCl$ drop by drop while shaking the tube
Stop when the pink color just disappears
Touch the test tube to feel the temperature
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understand that acids and bases cancel each other out to form salt and water
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why the color disappeared and the name of the reaction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Olfactory Indicators
activities_projects.objective: Identify acids and bases using the sense of smell
steps: Keep onion strips in a plastic bag with cloth strips overnight
Take two strips; put $HCl$ on one and $NaOH$ on the other
Rinse with water and smell both strips
Note which substance destroys the smell of the onion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines olfactory indicators and their use for visually impaired students
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct observation of smell retention in acidic/basic media (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Common Salt From the Kitchen
activities_projects.objective: Understand the chemical name and uses of Sodium Chloride
steps: Write the chemical formula for common salt
Research how it is used as a raw material for making other chemicals
List four chemicals derived from it (e.g., Bleaching powder, Baking soda)
Create a small table of these derivatives and one use for each
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the importance of common salt in the chemical industry
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the table and accuracy of chemical names (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water of Crystallization Demo
activities_projects.objective: Observe the effect of heating on copper sulphate crystals
steps: Heat a few crystals of blue copper sulphate in a dry boiling tube
Observe the color change to white
Notice the water droplets on the sides of the tube
Add 2-3 drops of water to the white powder and observe
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that some salts contain fixed water molecules in their structure
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct observation of the color returning to blue upon adding water (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Baking Soda vs. Washing Soda
steps: Research the chemical formulas for both ($NaHCO_3$ vs $Na_2CO_3 \cdot 10H_2O$)
Identify one use for baking soda in cooking
Identify one use for washing soda in cleaning
Write down which one is used in fire extinguishers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between similar-looking salts based on their chemical properties and uses
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate listing of formulas and practical uses (10 marks)
